ogTitle: Sabina River vs Salmon Gums: Climate Safety

heading: Sabina River vs Salmon Gums — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sabina River, Western Australia and Salmon Gums, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Salmon Gums has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Sabina River (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sabina River or Salmon Gums safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sabina River has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Salmon Gums scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sabina River scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Salmon Gums scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sabina River or Salmon Gums?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Salmon Gums has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
